data.name: Cantrips
data.description: At 1st level, you know two cantrips of your choice from the [druid spell list](#section-druid-spells). You learn additional druid cantrips of your choice at higher levels, as shown in the Cantrips Known column of the Druid table.

analysis.consistency: Consistent with standard 5e class feature presentation for Cantrips gained at level 1.
analysis.quality: High. The description clearly states the starting number of cantrips (two) and directs the user to the relevant spell list and progression table (Druid table).

mechanics.effect: Gain 2 cantrips from the Druid spell list.
mechanics.progression: Additional cantrips are gained at higher levels based on the Druid table progression.
mechanics.note: The `is_subclass_feature` being true seems potentially contradictory or misleading based on standard 5e rules, where gaining initial cantrips is typically a core class feature, not a subclass feature. If this data is for a specific variant or a custom system, this flag needs clarification. Assuming standard 5e interpretation, this flag should likely be false.

llm_audit.archivist_notes: The mechanical description is sound for a core class feature. The boolean flag `is_subclass_feature: true` appears inconsistent with the description which references the base 'Druid table', suggesting this is a core class feature, not specific to a subclass.
